The US economy added 187,000 non-farm payroll jobs in October 2025, slightly above the consensus estimate of 175,000, according to the Bureau of Labor Statistics. The unemployment rate held steady at 3.8%, while average hourly earnings rose 0.3% month-over-month, in line with forecasts. The Federal Reserve has signaled that it will keep its benchmark interest rate in the 5.25%–5.50% range until inflation shows a sustained return toward its 2% target. Equity markets responded positively to the report: the S&P 500 gained 0.7%, the Nasdaq Composite rose 0.9%, and the 10-year Treasury yield dipped 4 basis points to 4.61%. Analysts note that the labor market remains resilient despite elevated borrowing costs, suggesting the US economy may achieve a soft landing.
